coredata.dc:title: Redox-controlled synthesis of fluorescent polydopamine nanoparticles for label-free detection of glutathione
coredata.dc:creator: Luo, Jing-Song
Jin, You-Ping
Guo, Yu-Meng
Li, Qing
coredata.dc:description: Glutathione (GSH) plays critical roles in biological processes, which mainly maintain cellular redox homeostasis and resist oxidative damage, often be used as an important examination index for early human diseases. In this paper, fluorescent polydopamine nanoparticles (PDA NPs) are synthesized by a simple, and environment-friendly method with the aid of the CoOOH nanosheets. The proposed synthetic strategy with the merit of convenient, effective and ecofriendly, which offer a novel design for the preparation of fluorescent PDA NPs. Remarkably, the obtained fluorescent PDA NPs exhibit excellent chemical stability and photostability. However, when GSH were present, the fluorescence intensity of PDA NPs could be decreased through the reduction of GSH on CoOOH nanosheets, thus the as-prepared PDA NPs were successfully utilized as a label-free nanoprobe for sensitively and selectively sensing GSH. Under optimal conditions, the fluorometric signal is linear ranging from 10 μM to 500 μM GSH concentration with a detection limit of 3.25 μM. The detection platform also showed good potential application value in human serum samples with satisfactory recoveries.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Glutathione
CoOOH nanosheets
Fluorescent polydopamine nanoparticles
Redox
Label-free

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Sensing of GSH in human sera samples
Synthesis and characterization fluorescent PDA NPs
GSH detection based on fluorescent PDA NPs sensing strategy
Analysis of GSH for human serum sample

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (A) TEM image of the CoOOH nanosheets. (B) UV–vis absorption spectra of CoOOH nanosheets. (C) Fluorescence spectra of dopamine + CoOOH nanosheets (black line), dopamine (red line), CoOOH nanosheets (green line). (D) TEM image of the prepared PDA NPs. (For interpretation of the references to colour in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the web version of this article.)
(A) Fluorescence spectra of 2.5 mM dopamine solution reacting with different concentrations of CoOOH nanosheets (0–150 μg/mL). (B) Histogram drawn according to (A).

Fluorescence spectra responses of dopamine + CoOOH nanosheets (red line), GSH + dopamine + CoOOH nanosheets (black line). (For interpretation of the references to colour in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the web version of this article.)

(A) Fluorescence spectra of fluorescent PDA NPs obtained with different concentrations of GSH. (B) The corresponding fluorescence change values (F0 − F) versus GSH concentration (F0 and F are the fluorescence intensities of PDA NPs at peak with the existence and non-existence of GSH).
The specificity of the fluorescent PDA NPs based sensor against GSH and interferents. The concentration for GSH or other interferents are all 500 μΜ.
Schematic diagram of fluorescent PDA NPs for GSH sensing.

head.ce:title: Redox-controlled synthesis of fluorescent polydopamine nanoparticles for label-free detection of glutathione

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Hunan Key Laboratory of Biomedical Nanomaterials and Devices
College of Life Science and Chemistry

ce:footnote.ce:note-para: These authors have contributed equally to this work.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: In this paper, fluorescent polydopamine nanoparticles (PDA NPs) are synthesized by a simple, and environment-friendly method with the aid of the CoOOH nanosheets. The proposed synthetic strategy with the merit of convenient, effective and ecofriendly, which offer a novel design for the preparation of fluorescent PDA NPs. Remarkably, the obtained fluorescent PDA NPs exhibit excellent chemical stability and photostability. However, when GSH were present, the fluorescence intensity of PDA NPs could be decreased through the reduction of GSH on CoOOH nanosheets, thus the as-prepared PDA NPs were successfully utilized as a label-free nanoprobe for sensitively and selectively sensing GSH. Under optimal conditions, the fluorometric signal is linear ranging from 10 μM to 500 μM GSH concentration with a detection limit of 3.25 μM. The detection platform also showed good potential application value in human serum samples with satisfactory recoveries.

Glutathione (GSH) plays critical roles in biological processes, which mainly maintain cellular redox homeostasis and resist oxidative damage, often be used as an important examination index for early human diseases. In this paper, fluorescent polydopamine nanoparticles (PDA NPs) are synthesized by a simple, and environment-friendly method with the aid of the CoOOH nanosheets. The proposed synthetic strategy with the merit of convenient, effective and ecofriendly, which offer a novel design for the preparation of fluorescent PDA NPs. Remarkably, the obtained fluorescent PDA NPs exhibit excellent chemical stability and photostability. However, when GSH were present, the fluorescence intensity of PDA NPs could be decreased through the reduction of GSH on CoOOH nanosheets, thus the as-prepared PDA NPs were successfully utilized as a label-free nanoprobe for sensitively and selectively sensing GSH. Under optimal conditions, the fluorometric signal is linear ranging from 10 μM to 500 μM GSH concentration with a detection limit of 3.25 μM. The detection platform also showed good potential application value in human serum samples with satisfactory recoveries.

ce:section.ce:para: The coordination of oxidants and antioxidants in the human body play important roles in maintaining the function of normal metabolism and regulating cells [1]. Glutathione (GSH), a tripeptide containing sulfhydryl groups, which composed of glutamic acid, glycine and cysteine. As an antioxidant, biological GSH is also the most abundant non-protein thiol compound in all mammalian cells, and plays a vital role in immunity enhancement, oxygen free radical scavenging in the body, detoxification, and cell signal transduction. Many studies revealed that the imbalance of GSH metabolism is closely related to the occurrence of cancer, human immunodeficiency virus, Parkinson’s disease, Alzheimer's disease, Chronic liver disease, hypertension and other diseases [2]. Therefore, it is still of great significance to explore a sensitive strategy for GSH determination for disease diagnosis and medical treatment.

Up to now, a variety of analytical methods have been proposed for GSH sensing, including electrochemical methods [3–5], high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) [6], capillary electrophoresis [7], and surface-enhanced Raman scattering (SERS) [8]. However, these detection methods have deficiencies that cannot be ignored, such as high experiment cost, time-consuming, and complicated process. Compared with these technologies, fluorescence spectroscopy have been attracted wide attention because of its simple operation, high sensitivity and non-destructive advantages [9–12]. Especially, the development of fluorescent nanomaterials have received widespread attention because of it greatly promoted their application of fluorescent biosensors in biological analysis [13–16]. By comparison with organic dyes, nanomaterials possess the advantages such as narrow emission range, high photostability, large stokes shifts and long fluorescence lifetime [17]. Up to now, nanomaterials, including CdTe quantum dots [18] and upconversion nanoparticles [19] have been introduced for GSH detection. Nevertheless, the preparation procedures and reaction conditions of these nanoparticles might be sophisticated and harsh. Furthermore, considering the complex detection environment of GSH in serum, it is still urgent to exploit a nanomaterial with simple synthesis and excellent stability for GSH sensing in human serum sample.

Recently, fluorescent polymer nanoparticles, which exhibit extraordinary photostability under complex biological environment and ease of synthesis, have emerged as a brilliant label-free nanoprobe in the fields of biosensing and fluorescent imaging [20–23]. Dopamine is an abundant brain catecholamine neurotransmitter, which play an important role in regulating brain nerve function, usually act as a small molecule precursor for preparing polydopamine. In the alkali medium or oxidation pressure, the catechol group of dopamine catechol could be converted to quinones, and further self-assemble and polymerize into nanostructured polydopamine with inherent fluorescent properties [20]. A variety of fabrication methods have been reported for the controllable fabrication of fluorescent polydopamine nanoparticles. Zhang et al. firstly reported the preparation of fluorescent PDA NPs by using hydrogen peroxide treated with dopamine [24]. Tseng et al. developed the formation of fluorescent PDA by hydroxide radical degraded large PDA particles [25]. However, the involvement of high concentration of hazardous agent (up to 2 M hydrogen peroxide) may exist the hidden security problem. Yildirim’s group in situ synthesis of fluorescent PDA through incubation dopamine in sodium hydroxide solution for 3 h [26]. Despite such notable progress, a time-consuming and tedious synthesis process may largely limit further application. Thus, the development of a facile, rapid and environmental-friendly method of synthesizing fluorescent PDA NPs and explore more attractive detection strategy is still considerable.

CoOOH nanosheets are typical two-dimensional transition metal nanomaterials with hexagonal layered structure [27]. Because they showed a wide absorption spectrum, large surface area and excellent electron transfer rate, CoOOH nanosheets are often used as efficient quenchers for fluorescent dyes or fluorescent nanoparticle [28]. Meanwhile, CoOOH nanosheets are also an oxidant that can react with some reducing agents and transform into Co(II), which is also used in detection of biological reducing substances [29]. Inspired by this, the synthesis of fluorescent polydopamine nanoparticles by using the oxidation ability of CoOOH nanosheets may provide a new possibility for the construction of GSH detection platform. Herein, we reported an environmentally-friendly and low-cost in situ synthesizing fluorescent polydopamine nanoparticles method employing CoOOH nanosheets as oxidant, and further developed a convenient nanosensor for sensitively and selectively detecting GSH, as depicted in Scheme 1 . Dopamine could be rapidly oxidated to its quinone derivative and then self-polymerized into fluorescent PDA NPs when CoOOH nanosheets are present as oxidants. The as-prepared fluorescent PDA NPs solution was light yellow with good water solubility and exhibit a strong fluorescence emission wavelength at 490 nm with the excitation wavelength of 400 nm. In the presence of GSH, CoOOH nanosheets can be dissolved into Co2+ by the reduction of GSH (CoOOH + GSH + 2H+ → Co2+ + GSSG + 2H2O), thus destroy the oxidative ability of CoOOH nanosheets towards dopamine and hinder the formation of the fluorescent PDA NPs. Accordingly, the fluorescence signal of PDA NPs was decreased with increasing GSH concentration. Based on the redox reaction in situ modulated synthesis of fluorescent PDA NPs, a label-free GSH detecting platform is constructed by observing the changes in fluorescence intensity of PDA NPs.

Dopamine, L-glutathione (GSH), sodium hypochlorite (NaClO), sodium hydroxide (NaOH), cobalt chloride (CoCl2), homocysteine (Hcy), alanine (Ala), histidine (His), glycine (Gly), glutamicacid (Glu), glutamin (Gln), cysteine (Cys), gamma linolenic acid (Gla) and vitamin C (Vc) were obtained from China National Pharmaceutical Group Corp. Ultra-pure water is used throughout the experiment with the electric resistance value > 18.2 MΩ. Human serum samples were provided by Zhuzhou Central Hospital (Zhuzhou, China).

Fluorescence spectrum were obtained by F-7100 fluorescence spectrometer (Hitachi Ltd., Tokyo, Japan). The excitation wavelength was set at 400 nm and collected fluorescence data from 420 nm to 650 nm. UV–vis absorption spectra were measured by Lambda 750 s UV–vis spectrophotometer (PerkinElmer, USA). Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) was performed by H-7500 transmission electron microscope (Hitachi Ltd., Tokyo, Japan) at an accelerating voltage of 80 kV.

The preparation of CoOOH nanosheets was according to the previous preparation process [27] with a little modification. 1.0 M sodium hydroxide (NaOH) 125 μL was added to 500 μL CoCl2 solution (10 mM) and sonicated for 1 min. Then, 25 µL of NaClO (0.9 M) was introduced to the above system and sonicated for 30 min under 25 ℃. Subsequently, the above mixture solution was centrifuged 10 min at a speed of 16,000 rpm, and rinse with water 4 times. Subsequently, the prepared CoOOH nanosheets were stored at 4 °C for future use.

Firstly, 10 μL 10 mM PB buffer solution (pH 7.4), 75 μL ultra-pure water and 10 μL CoOOH nanosheets solution (0.75 mg/mL) were added into centrifugal tube and mixed. Then, 5 μL dopamine solution (50 mM) was introduced and reacted for 1 h to formation of fluorescent PDA NPs. Thereafter, 6 μL HCl (0.2 M) was introduced into the above system to end the polymerization.

Firstly, a series concentration of GSH solution, 10 μL CoOOH nanosheets, 65 μL ultra-pure water and 10 μL phosphate buffer solution (pH 7.4) was added into centrifuge tube and incubated for 10 min. After that, 5 μL dopamine solution (50 mM) was introduced into the above mixed solution and incubate for 1 h at room temperature. Subsequently, 6 μL 0.2 M HCl was introduced to terminate the polymerization and then performed fluorescence spectrum analysis.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Sensing of GSH in human sera samples
ce:section.ce:para: The human sera sample were acquired from healthy volunteers. Firstly, 500 μL serum samples were pretreated with 1.5 mL trichloroacetic acid for precipitation of protein in the sera. After that, the serum samples were centrifuged at 12,000 rpm for 10 min at 4 °C, then the resulting supernatant was further diluted 50 times with PB buffer (pH 7.4) and mixed with fluorescent PDA NPs detecting system. After that, various amount of GSH were added into the above sera to acquire spiked sample for recovery tests. The following operation process was carried out under the same experiment condition.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synthesis and characterization fluorescent PDA NPs
ce:section.ce:para: The as-prepared CoOOH nanosheets was firstly to ensure successful synthesis. The TEM image of the CoOOH nanosheets (Fig. 1 A) clearly showed that its morphology was basically hexagonal and two-dimensional nanosheet, which was matched with the previous reports [28]. It could be seen from the UV–vis absorption spectrum that the CoOOH nanosheets had a maximum absorption peak at about 410 nm, with a broad absorption in the range of 300–800 nm (Fig. 1B). The FT-IR spectrum of the CoOOH nanosheets indicates (Fig. S1) that the broad band at 3440 cm−1 was assigned to the bond stretching vibration of the OH group and the deformation vibration of the OH group had a narrow peak at 2925 cm−1. The peak at 1634 cm−1 showed the CoO bond of the CoOOH nanosheets crystal structure, while peak at 580 cm−1 correspond to CoO2– complex in the oxide [34]. As shown in XRD spectrum (Fig. S2), the peak of CoOOH phase based on the standard card (JCPDS 07-0169) corresponds to cobalt phase (JCPDS 05-0727), and the position of the diffraction peak was consistent with the previous report. It verified the successful preparation of CoOOH nanosheets.

Based on the typical fluorescence spectrum in Fig. 1C, a strong characteristic peak at 490 nm appeared after mixing dopamine solution with the CoOOH nanosheets (black line) for 60 min. In contrast, only dopamine (red line) or CoOOH nanosheets (green lines) was present, almost no fluorescence signal was observed. This result indicated that dopamine was oxidized into quinones by CoOOH nanosheets, and further spontaneously formed the fluorescent PDA NPs. The morphology of PDA NPs was further characterized by TEM image (Fig. 1D), the size distribution of PDA NPs was relatively uniform, and the average particle size was about 5 nm. It was speculated that the main reason may be that the oxidation synthesis strategy resulting in a number of carbonyl group and thus preventing the formation of π-π interaction during the polymerization of the quinone. Therefore, the PDA NPs can exhibit strong fluorescence signal and uniform nano size. We further investigated the optical properties and stability of fluorescent PDA NPs. According to Fig. S3, the maximum excitation wavelength of the prepared fluorescent PDA NPs was 400 nm and a maximum emission peak was obtained at 490 nm, while the maximum emission wavelength of fluorescent PDA NPs hardly changed with variable excitation wavelength (Fig. S4). We also analyzed the composition of PDA NPs by EDS measurement as shown in Fig. S5, it was obvious that elements including C, N and O were present in the fluorescent PDA NPs. Fig. S6 displayed the FT-IR spectrum of PDA NPs, the absorption band at 1636 cm−1 and 3443 cm−1 was mainly ascribe to the stretching vibration of aromatic CC bonds of indole and OH group. The peak at 2925 cm−1 was attributed to the deformation vibration of the OH group. These results indicated that the fluorescent PDA NPs were prepared indeed.

Before utilizing the strategy for label-free sensing GSH, a series of experiments condition were systematically investigated to acquire the optimal response performance. The dose of CoOOH nanosheets may largely influence the fluorescence intensity of PDA NPs, therefore the dose of oxidizing agent was further studied. As shown in Fig. 2 A, fixed dopamine concentration at 2.5 mM, with the dose of CoOOH nanosheets increased, the fluorescence intensity at 490 nm of fluorescent dopamine nanoparticles also increased within the range of 0–75 μg/mL. However, when the dose of CoOOH continues increased, the fluorescence signal reduced. The reason might be ascribed to excessive oxidants lead to the aggregation of large-scale PDA NPs, which induce fluorescence quenching [20]. Therefore, we used concentration of 75 μg∙mL−1 CoOOH nanosheets for further research. The oxidation and polymerization reached the maximum fluorescence intensity within 60 min and tend to be stable (Fig. S7), which verified that fluorescent PDA NPs can be prepared rapidly. In order to obtain a stable fluorescence intensity value of PDA NPs, hydrochloric acid was introduced to the mixture to terminate the polymerization, the concentration of terminator was also examined. As shown in Fig. S8, after 60 min of oxidation and polymerization, various amount of HCl was added into the system immediately. It can be observed that the fluorescence signal decreased with the increasing concentration of HCl over the range of 2–6 mM and almost keep unchanged with the higher doses. Therefore, 6 mM HCl was selected in the following polymerization termination reaction. We further checked the fluorescent intensity of PDA NPs after adding HCl (Fig. S9), the fluorescence signal of PDA NPs hardly changed over the time, which verified the addition of HCl indeed terminate the oxidation and polymerization reaction. Next, the chemical stability and photostability of fluorescent PDA NPs toward various operation environments were evaluated. As revealed in Fig. S10, the fluorescence intensities of PDA NPs basically keep constant within the wide pH range from 3 to 12. Afterwards, the effect of ionic strength on the chemical stability was also explored by altering the concentration of sodium chloride. Fig. S11 showed the addition of sodium chloride with a concentration ranged from 0 to 500 mM, the fluorescent PDA NPs still displayed a stable fluorescence intensity, indicating their significantly chemical stability of in the condition of high ionic strength. Meanwhile, when the prepared fluorescent PDA NPs stored at a 4 °C refrigerator for seven days, there was no obvious changes in the fluorescence signal (Fig. S12). Therefore, these results demonstrated that the fluorescent PDA NPs had excellent chemical stability and photostability and hold the potential for bioanalysis in complex environments.

Inspired by the important part of CoOOH nanosheets in regulating fabrication of fluorescent PDA NPs, we speculate that redox reaction with GSH may influence the dose of CoOOH nanosheets and alter the fluorescence intensity of PDA NPs. Therefore, we further investigated the redox regulation synthesis of fluorescent PDA NPs as sensing strategy for determination of GSH. We firstly performed a typical fluorescence spectrum measurement to verify the feasibility of this detection platform to determination of GSH. According to Fig. 3 , in the absence of GSH, PDA NPs exhibited a strong fluorescence signal at 490 nm (red line). However, when 500 μM GSH was added into the system, the fluorescence intensity of PDA NPs was drastically reduced (black line). The reason for this phenomenon was ascribed that the CoOOH nanosheets were reduced to Co2+ with the existence of GSH, and the oxidization and polymerization of dopamine was inhibited, thus PDA NPs with weak fluorescence intensity were obtained. In order to further verify the mechanism of PDA NPs for GSH sensing, UV–vis absorption spectrum was also interrogated as shown in Fig. S13. As for the characteristic peak, there was two strong absorptions ranging from 200 nm to 300 nm, which was associated with the phenol group of dopamine (orange line). Compared with the system contained only dopamine, there was a new broad absorption peak at ∼420 nm which was assigned to the formation of polydopamine (yellow line). After the addition of GSH to the mixture of CoOOH nanosheets and dopamine, the characteristics absorption peak of CoOOH nanosheets disappeared at 420 nm (green line), suggesting CoOOH nanosheets were reduced by GSH and the formation of PDA NPs was inhibited. These results demonstrated that the feasibility of utilize redox regulating the formation of fluorescent PDA NPs for GSH detection.

ce:section.ce:section-title: GSH detection based on fluorescent PDA NPs sensing strategy
ce:section.ce:para: The capacity of quantitative sensing GSH using redox controlling fabrication of fluorescent PDA NPs was investigated under the optimal experiment conditions. As observed from Fig. 4 A, the fluorescence signal of PDA NPs gradually decreased as the GSH concentration increased in the range from 0 μM to 1000 μM. It showed a good linear relationship between (F0-F) and GSH concentration ranged in 10–500 μΜ with a correlation coefficient of 0.9967 (Fig. 4B). According to the rule of 3σ, the detection limit (LOD) for GSH detection was calculated to be 3.25 μΜ. Also, the sensitivity of this method was comparable to the previous strategy for GSH sensing (Table S1) [30–32]. Importantly, this developed redox controlling synthesis of fluorescent PDA NPs probe had the advantages of low cost, simplicity and good stability.

To verify the selectivity of the proposed strategy, the effect of other small molecular and ions were investigated. We chose some interfering substances such as metal ions, amino acids and vitamin C to compare with the GSH response performance. As shown in Fig. 5 , excepted for GSH displayed a significantly decreased fluorescence signals, the influence of other substance on fluorescence intensity was almost negligible. Remarkably, the reductive substance such as Cys (Cysteine), homocysteine (Hcy) and vitamin C can react with CoOOH nanosheets and affect the determination of GSH. However, the concentration of Cys, Hcy and vitamin C in human whole blood were 250 μΜ, 15 μΜ and 50 μΜ, respectively [33], which is much lower than GSH and do not affect the determination of GSH. Thus, it can be resolved by dilution the serum samples for the detection of GSH in real sample. Therefore, this result confirmed that fluorescent PDA NPs have excellent selectivity toward GSH.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Analysis of GSH for human serum sample
ce:section.ce:para: To verify the application of the proposed method for measuring GSH in real sample, human serum sample were spiked with different concentration (10 μΜ, 25 μΜ, 50 μΜ, 100 μΜ) of GSH. As shown in Table S2, satisfactory recoveries were obtained (96.48–101.70%), and the relative standard deviation was ranging from 2.9% to 3.4%. Therefore, the above results indicate that fluorescent PDA NPs sensing platform has potential for practical sample analysis.

In summary, an easy-operation and facile approach for preparing intrinsic fluorescent PDA NPs by using CoOOH nanosheets as an oxidant. Based on the redox reaction between GSH and CoOOH nanosheets, in situ modulation synthesis fluorescent PDA NPs can be achieved. This mechanism has been successfully applied to monitor changes of the concentration of GSH. In addition, the practicability of fluorescent PDA NPs for sensing GSH was further survived through the quantitative analysis of GSH in human serum sample and the results was satisfactory. Although the detection time is long and the detection limit for GSH is not low, the synthetic method of fluorescent PDANPs reported in this paper has the advantages of low cost, simple operation and cost-effectiveness. The specific recognition of GSH regulates the redox reaction and controls the synthesis of fluorescent PDANPs to ensure the selectivity of the sensor, thus realizing selective and label-free detection of GSH. Therefore, this convenient strategy could provide a promising platform for the label-free detection of GSH and hold a potential in GSH-related basic research and disease diagnosis.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (21705042), Hunan Provincial Natural Science Foundation (2021JJ40169).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.inoche.2022.109307.

ce:section.ce:para:
The following are the Supplementary data to this article:
